metadata.name: Phase 1 English Communication Practice Content Pack
metadata.intendedUse: Static GitHub Pages MVP for employee English practice
notes: No AI integration required for Phase 1.
Content is generic but workplace and interview oriented.
Fields include futureTags so later phases can add speech recognition, AI feedback, role-specific routing, and analytics.

shadowing.text: Could you please repeat that?
shadowing.tip: Use this when you need to confirm meaning or ask for repetition.

shadowing.text: Could you please say that again more slowly?
shadowing.tip: Use this when you need to confirm meaning or ask for repetition.

shadowing.text: I’m sorry, I didn’t catch the last part.
shadowing.tip: Use this when you need to confirm meaning or ask for repetition.

shadowing.text: Could you please explain what you mean?
shadowing.tip: Use this when you need to confirm meaning or ask for repetition.

shadowing.text: Just to confirm, are you asking about my previous role?

shadowing.text: Could you please give me a moment?

sentenceBuilder.title: Explaining previous work
steps: I worked.
I worked on reports.
I worked on weekly reports.
I worked on weekly performance reports.
I worked on weekly performance reports for multiple clients.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Describing responsibilities
steps: I was responsible.
I was responsible for checking data.
I was responsible for checking data every day.
I was responsible for checking data and updating the team.
I was responsible for checking data and updating the team before the deadline.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Introducing your background
steps: I have experience.
I have experience in customer support.
I have experience in customer support and communication.
I have experience in customer support and communication with clients.
I have experience in customer support and communication with clients through chat and email.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Explaining a strength
steps: I am organized.
I am organized with my tasks.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines, especially when work is busy.
I am organized with my tasks and deadlines, especially when work is busy and priorities change.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Asking for repetition
steps: Please repeat that.
Could you please repeat that?
Could you please repeat the last part?
Could you please repeat the last part more slowly?
Could you please repeat the last part more slowly so I can answer correctly?
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Checking understanding
steps: I understood the point.
I think I understood the point.
I think I understood the main point.
I think I understood the main point, but I want to confirm one detail.
I think I understood the main point, but I want to confirm one detail before I answer.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Updating progress
steps: I checked it.
I checked the file.
I checked the file and found an issue.
I checked the file and found an issue that needs review.
I checked the file and found an issue that needs review before we continue.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Explaining a delay
steps: I need time.
I need a little more time.
I need a little more time to check this.
I need a little more time to check this properly.
I need a little more time to check this properly and avoid giving the wrong answer.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Showing empathy
steps: I understand.
I understand your concern.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this for you.
I understand your concern and I’m checking this for you as carefully as possible.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Offering help
steps: I can help.
I can help you.
I can help you with that.
I can help you with that and guide you through the next step.
I can help you with that and guide you through the next step if you can share a few details.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Explaining confidence
steps: I can do this.
I can do this task.
I can do this task with guidance.
I can do this task with guidance and regular practice.
I can do this task with guidance and regular practice, and I am willing to learn.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Learning new tools
steps: I can learn.
I can learn new tools.
I can learn new tools quickly.
I can learn new tools quickly if I get clear instructions.
I can learn new tools quickly if I get clear instructions and enough practice.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Present perfect practice
steps: I have worked.
I have worked on reports.
I have worked on reports before.
I have worked on reports before in my previous role.
I have worked on reports before in my previous role and I am comfortable with similar tasks.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Past simple practice
steps: I completed it.
I completed the task.
I completed the task yesterday.
I completed the task yesterday and shared the update.
I completed the task yesterday and shared the update with the team.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Starting a conversation
steps: Good morning.
Good morning, how are you?
Good morning, how are you today?
Good morning, I hope you’re doing well today.
Good morning, I hope you’re doing well today and thank you for speaking with me.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Answering why this role 16
steps: I am interested.
I am interested in this role.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills and goals.
I am interested in this role because it matches my skills and gives me room to grow.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Discussing weakness 17
steps: I am improving.
I am improving my English.
I am improving my English speaking skills.
I am improving my English speaking skills through daily practice.
I am improving my English speaking skills through daily practice and structured interview preparation.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Describing tools 18
steps: I used tools.
I used online tools.
I used online tools for daily work.
I used online tools for daily work, reporting, and communication.
I used online tools for daily work, reporting, and communication with team members.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Explaining teamwork 19
steps: I worked with a team.
I worked with a small team.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks and shared updates regularly.
I worked with a small team on daily tasks and shared updates regularly to keep everyone aligned.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Clarifying deadline 20
steps: When is it due?
Could you tell me when it is due?
Could you tell me when this task is due?
Could you tell me when this task is due so I can plan properly?
Could you tell me when this task is due so I can plan properly and prioritize the work?
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Requesting details 21
steps: Please share details.
Could you please share the details?
Could you please share the details of the task?
Could you please share the details of the task and the expected outcome?
Could you please share the details of the task and the expected outcome so I can complete it correctly?
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Requesting review 22
steps: Please review this.
Could you please review this?
Could you please review this when you have time?
Could you please review this when you have time and let me know if changes are needed?
Could you please review this when you have time and let me know if any changes are needed before submission?
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Completed task 23
steps: It is done.
The task is done.
The task is done and updated.
The task is done and updated in the file.
The task is done and updated in the file for your review.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Avoiding guessing 24
steps: I am not sure.
I am not fully sure.
I am not fully sure, so I will check.
I am not fully sure, so I will check before answering.
I am not fully sure, so I will check before answering to avoid giving incorrect information.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Acknowledging issue 25
steps: I see the issue.
I see the issue you mentioned.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking it.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking the details now.
I see the issue you mentioned and I’m checking the details now so I can guide you correctly.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: Handling pressure 26
steps: I stay calm.
I try to stay calm.
I try to stay calm under pressure.
I try to stay calm under pressure and focus on the next step.
I try to stay calm under pressure and focus on the next step instead of rushing.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

sentenceBuilder.title: For vs since 27
steps: I have worked here.
I have worked here for one year.
I have worked here since last year.
I have worked here for one year and learned many things.
I have worked here for one year and learned many things from my team.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

steps: Nice to meet you.
It’s nice to meet you.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for your time.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for taking the time to speak with me.
It’s nice to meet you and thank you for taking the time to speak with me today.
sentenceBuilder.tip: Repeat each step out loud before moving to the longer version.

interviewPractice.question: Tell me about yourself.
framework: Start with your field or role.
End with what you want to do next.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: I have experience in customer communication and operations support. In my previous role, I worked on daily tasks, reporting, and coordination with team members. I am comfortable using online tools, following instructions, and learning new systems. I am now looking for a role where I can continue improving and contribute to a professional team.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not give your full life story. Keep the answer focused on work experience.

interviewPractice.question: Walk me through your experience.
framework: Start from your most relevant role.
Mention tools or processes you used.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: In my previous role, I supported daily operations and communication tasks. I was responsible for checking information, updating records, and coordinating with my team. I used tools like Excel, email, and online systems. This experience helped me improve my attention to detail and communication skills.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Avoid listing every job. Focus on the most relevant experience.

interviewPractice.question: Why are you interested in this role?
framework: Connect the role to your skills.
Mention what you want to learn.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: I am interested in this role because it matches my communication and problem-solving skills. I enjoy working in structured environments where I can learn clear processes and improve through practice. I also believe this role will help me grow while contributing to the team’s goals.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not say only that you need a job.

interviewPractice.question: What are your strengths?
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: My main strengths are attention to detail, willingness to learn, and communication. For example, I try to check my work carefully before submitting it, and I ask questions when something is unclear. These habits help me avoid mistakes and improve my performance.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not give a long list without examples.

interviewPractice.question: What is one area you are trying to improve?
framework: Choose a real but manageable weakness.
Explain what you are doing to improve.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: One area I am working on is speaking more confidently in English. I can understand and write better than I speak sometimes, so I am practicing speaking every day. I am improving by repeating phrases, listening carefully, and preparing answers before interviews or meetings.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not choose a weakness that is essential to the job unless you explain improvement.

interviewPractice.question: Why should we hire you?
framework: Match your skills to the job.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: You should hire me because I am reliable, willing to learn, and focused on doing work carefully. I ask questions, follow instructions, and improve through practice. I believe I can support the team and grow into the role.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not sound overconfident without evidence.

interviewPractice.question: Tell me about a project you worked on.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: One project I worked on involved organizing information and preparing regular updates for the team. My role was to check the data, update the file, and communicate any issues. This helped the team stay organized and taught me the importance of accuracy.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not describe the project without explaining your own role.

interviewPractice.question: How would you handle an upset customer?
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: I would stay calm and listen carefully to understand the customer’s concern. I would acknowledge the issue, ask for the necessary details, and explain the next step clearly. If I cannot solve it myself, I would escalate it to the right person.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not argue with the customer.

interviewPractice.question: What do you do when instructions are unclear?
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: When instructions are unclear, I try not to guess. I ask specific questions and repeat what I understood to confirm. Once the requirement is clear, I continue with the task carefully.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not continue without clarifying.

interviewPractice.question: How do you manage pressure?
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: I manage pressure by staying calm and breaking the work into smaller steps. I try to understand what is most urgent and focus on that first. If there is a risk of delay, I communicate early so the team can plan properly.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not say pressure never affects you.

interviewPractice.question: How would you start a client call?
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: I would start by greeting the client politely and introducing myself. For example, I might say, 'Good morning, thank you for joining the call. My name is ___, and I’m happy to discuss this with you today.'
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not start too casually.

interviewPractice.question: Explain the difference between 'for' and 'since'.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: We use 'for' when we talk about a length of time, like 'for two years.' We use 'since' when we talk about the starting point, like 'since 2022.' For example, 'I have worked here for two years' and 'I have worked here since 2022.'
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not say 'since two years'.

framework: Match your skills to the job.
interviewPractice.sampleAnswer: You should hire me because I am reliable, willing to learn, and focused on doing work carefully. I ask questions, follow instructions, and improve through practice. I believe I can support the team and grow into the role.
selfReviewChecklist: Did I answer the question directly?
Did I use complete sentences?
Did I give at least one specific detail or example?
Was my answer clear and concise?
interviewPractice.commonMistake: Do not sound overconfident without evidence.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Could you walk me through your previous experience?
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker asking?
options: They want you to explain your past work step by step.
They are asking for a home address.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The phrase 'walk me through' means explain step by step.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Tell me about a time you handled a difficult task.
listeningComprehension.question: What should you talk about?
options: A specific challenging task and how you handled it.
listeningComprehension.explanation: They want a specific example, not a general statement.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Could you clarify the last point?
listeningComprehension.question: What does the speaker need?
options: They need the last point explained more clearly.
They want to end the call.
listeningComprehension.explanation: Clarify means make something clearer.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Could you please send the details by email?
listeningComprehension.question: What is being requested?
listeningComprehension.explanation: The speaker wants the information in writing.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I have completed the first part of the task.
listeningComprehension.question: What does this mean?
options: Only the first part is done.
listeningComprehension.explanation: First part completed means there is more work remaining.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Thank you for your patience while I check this for you.
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker doing?
options: Asking the customer to wait politely.
listeningComprehension.explanation: This is a polite support phrase during checking.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I may need a little more context before I answer.
listeningComprehension.question: What does the speaker need?
listeningComprehension.explanation: Context means background or additional information.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I can learn the process quickly if I get clear instructions.
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker saying?
options: They are willing to learn with guidance.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The sentence shows adaptability and willingness.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today.
listeningComprehension.question: When would someone say this?
options: At the beginning or end of a professional conversation.
listeningComprehension.explanation: This is a polite professional phrase.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I have worked here for two years.
listeningComprehension.question: What does 'for two years' show?
listeningComprehension.explanation: Use 'for' with a length of time.

listeningComprehension.audioText: What kind of work environment do you prefer?
listeningComprehension.question: What should you describe?
listeningComprehension.explanation: The question asks about work style and environment.

listeningComprehension.audioText: What were your main responsibilities?
listeningComprehension.question: What should you explain?
options: The key tasks you handled in your role.
listeningComprehension.explanation: Responsibilities means tasks you were expected to do.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Are you referring to the deadline or the task details?
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker clarifying?
options: Which specific point the other person means.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The speaker is narrowing down the meaning.

listeningComprehension.audioText: May I take a moment to check that?
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker asking for?
options: A short time to verify information.
listeningComprehension.explanation: They need time to check.

listeningComprehension.audioText: The file has been updated for your review.
listeningComprehension.question: What happened?
options: The file was updated and is ready to be reviewed.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The update is complete and ready for review.

listeningComprehension.audioText: There may be a small misunderstanding here.
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker suggesting?
options: Something may not have been understood correctly.
listeningComprehension.explanation: A misunderstanding means confusion between people.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I will escalate this to the appropriate team.
listeningComprehension.question: What does escalate mean here?
options: Send the issue to the right team for help.
listeningComprehension.explanation: Escalate means pass the issue to someone better able to handle it.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I try to stay calm under pressure.
listeningComprehension.question: What does this describe?
options: How the speaker handles stressful situations.
listeningComprehension.explanation: It describes work behavior under pressure.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Have a great rest of your day.
listeningComprehension.question: What type of phrase is this?
listeningComprehension.explanation: It closes a conversation warmly.

listeningComprehension.audioText: I worked on this yesterday.
listeningComprehension.question: What tense is this?
listeningComprehension.explanation: 'Worked' with yesterday is past simple.

listeningComprehension.audioText: What kind of work environment do you prefer?
listeningComprehension.question: What should you describe?
listeningComprehension.explanation: The question asks about work style and environment.

listeningComprehension.audioText: What were your main responsibilities?
listeningComprehension.question: What should you explain?
options: The key tasks you handled in your role.
listeningComprehension.explanation: Responsibilities means tasks you were expected to do.

listeningComprehension.audioText: Are you referring to the deadline or the task details?
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker clarifying?
options: Which specific point the other person means.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The speaker is narrowing down the meaning.

listeningComprehension.audioText: May I take a moment to check that?
listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker asking for?
options: A short time to verify information.
listeningComprehension.explanation: They need time to check.

listeningComprehension.audioText: The file has been updated for your review.
listeningComprehension.question: What happened?
options: The file was updated and is ready to be reviewed.
listeningComprehension.explanation: The update is complete and ready for review.

listeningComprehension.question: What is the speaker suggesting?
options: Something may not have been understood correctly.
listeningComprehension.explanation: A misunderstanding means confusion between people.
